item.title: Fascinating yet intimidating
item.content:encoded: This is beyond interesting, especially with all the possibilities for this. Especially with those extended test logs. This idea works perfectly for an SCP as well.

item.title: An Interesting Concept
item.content:encoded: I really enjoyed reading this and I think it has just enough detail to make it interesting but not so much that it becomes long-winded. However, I must say SCP-4072-Alpha kind of ruined the feel. +1 upvote nonetheless.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Novote from me for now. I like the general concept, but the spooky monster at the end felt a little tropey and I didn't feel like it added much to the article.

Also, as a style thing I feel Foundation should be capitalized, and in the first test log having a "Download Speed" expressed in minutes felt a bit confusing. Normally speed would be measured in Bytes/second, so perhaps "Download Duration" would be a more accurate name for the category.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Victims of SCP-4072-X instances downloaded on the "Very High" quality setting, specifically D-Class personnel, are to be released from foundation custody, as they no longer pose a threat to the populace.

Okay, I know it says containment procedures are being revised at the end, but wouldn't it be common sense to run another D-Class through a Genocide run on Very High, just to make sure they have the cause and effect right?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Impressively intriguing concept along with references to video games that don't make me cringe. Good stuff all around.
+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Oh, I like this. I like this a lot. +1

item.title: Nice idea
item.content:encoded: The idea is a cool concept and seems like it has lots of potential side effects/differences with the games and I request permission to start on the test log

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: People were downvoting it, and claimed it made the original look bad.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: And I wanted to add a little story about a researcher downloading Fire Emblem: Sacred Stones, getting a fan-edited version, and being utterly convinced of the superiority of hexadecimal numbering systems….

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It got deleted… Oh well.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Done.
It was deleted

Edit: It's been a while now, can we get a reason why the experiment log was deleted?

▼ Update
▲ Update

Well, at least we got a reason for its deletion. it's still sad to me since there could have been many interesting logs there that further explain some aspects of the SCP such as:

What are SCP-4072's specific requirements for something to be considered gaming software?

To which extent SCP improve the narrative of a story?

What's the story about the ghost-like entity?

In which way does quality settings affect the effects of anomalous software?

How does it work with online games or MMOs?

To which extent can SCP-4072-X instances affect people?

And many more.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You can make a collaboration log for your own work, that's fine. You'll probably have to prune it yourself though, we have our hands full with 914 and the others.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Will do! I’ll probably consult a mod or an admin first to make sure they approve, though.

item.title: good stuff
item.content:encoded:
I like this 914-but-with-videogames idea you have. Now I'm curious what would happen if you downloaded an X game on the very high setting.
+1
game machine demon yes

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It's beautiful… Please give us an Experiment Log that people can add to… I have a feeling this could be another 914.

+1

item.title: Technical Captain Post-op Info - Open
item.content:encoded:
Please wait for the tag team to tag your article if you don't k is what you're doing.

I've cleared your tags for now.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thank you, and fixed!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I really like this one, although I do agree with Kanes suggestion about how its slightly absurd that the foundation would be in kahoots with the games industry without any real cover.

Otherwise, its a nicely written, kept me hooked throughout. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
All the content on Kisspng.com is either submitted to Kisspng.com by email or is readily available in various places on the Internet and believed to be in public domain.

- Kisspng

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: In my opinion really well executed for an SCP of such concept. Its a +1 from me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I love this except for the fact the foundation can just call up the heads of video game companies and openly discuss SCPs. Maybe add something about a ruse of an important CEO, just anything to get away from Sakurai being buddies with Clef.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Honestly, you should have waited for some critique from more experienced writers on the Wiki. In future, if you're having trouble getting crits on your draft, head over to the Butterfly Squad Roster to PM a Crit Staff member whose skills line up with themes in your draft.

Having looked over it again, it's good enough to change my downvote to a novote. However, do you have express permission to use the copyrighted image from kisspng.com?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is really interesting. I'm surprised you managed to make this work as well as you did. It is perhaps a bit long winded, but I didnt find any of the information particularly unnecessary. It's a shaky +1 from me, but an upvote nonetheless.

item.title: So, here's my new SCP.
item.content:encoded:
I hope everyone likes it this time around. I wrote up an initial draft, but after being told that it was kind of a stale idea, I reworked it into something newer and more spooky in my eyes with the help of the Ideas and Brainstorming Forum. Thanks, guys!

I made all the logos and advertisements, but I got the game controller clipart from here. Big thanks to whoever made that.

I hope to contribute to this wiki more and more as my writing style progresses and gets better. This is a really cool place full of really cool people, and I hope to be part of this huge collective of writing genius.

Credits:

GamePal Controller Icon: https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Xbox360-controller.jpg (albeit edited in this article, it's still the source image.)
